description: Foundational OOP concepts: classes, objects, constructors, destructors, access specifiers, this pointer, static members, and member functions. Many examples for beginners.

sections.title: What is OOP?
sections.definition: Object-Oriented Programming organizes code around 'objects' that contain data (attributes) and functions (methods). It models real-world entities.
sections.analogy: A Car is an object with attributes (color, model, speed) and methods (accelerate, brake).

sections.title: Classes and Objects
sections.definition: A class is a blueprint. An object is an instance of a class. Class defines data and behavior; object uses them.
sections.output:
Hi, I'm Alice, age 20
Hi, I'm Bob, age 22

sections.title: Access Specifiers
sections.definition: public: accessible anywhere. private: only inside the class. protected: inside class and derived classes. Encapsulation hides internal details.

sections.title: Constructors
sections.definition: A constructor is a special member function called automatically when an object is created. Same name as class, no return type. Can be overloaded.

sections.title: Constructor Initialization List
sections.definition: Initialization list initializes member variables before constructor body runs. More efficient than assignment, required for const and reference members.

sections.title: Destructors
sections.definition: Destructor is called when object is destroyed (goes out of scope or delete). Name ~Class, no parameters, no return. Used for cleanup (free memory, close files).
sections.output:
Resource acquired
Using resource
Resource released
After block

sections.title: this Pointer
sections.definition: this is a pointer to the current object. Used inside member functions to access the object's members, especially when parameter names shadow member names.

sections.title: Static Members
sections.definition: Static members belong to the class, not individual objects. One copy shared across all objects. Static functions can only access static members.
sections.output:
Object ID: 1
Object ID: 2
Object ID: 3
Total objects: 3

sections.title: Const Member Functions
sections.definition: Const member functions promise not to modify the object. They can be called on const objects. Mark with const after parameter list.
